title: South Africa names apartheid-era politician as new ambassador to the US

originalTitle: Three years of messages at once - a chronicle of Sudan's war pours in as trapped reporter's phone turns on
title: A Reporter's Big Surprise: Messages from Three Years!
content:
Hello, My Wonderful Friends!

Today, I have a very important story to share with you from a country called Sudan. Sudan is a big and beautiful country right here in Africa, just like many of our homes!

Sometimes, big sad things happen in the world. And a very big sad thing, like a huge fight, has been happening in Sudan for a very, very long time. It's called a war, and it has been going on for four whole years. That's a lot of birthdays and a lot of time for children to miss school and play!

Meet Mohamed, Our Brave Storyteller

Our story today is about a very kind and brave man named Mohamed Suleiman. Mohamed is a special kind of helper. He is a reporter. Do you know what a reporter does?

A reporter is like a super-duper storyteller for grown-ups and kids alike! They listen to people, they see what is happening in the world, and then they tell everyone the news. They help us understand what is going on, even in faraway places.

Mohamed wanted to tell everyone about the big fight in Sudan. He wanted to share the true stories of the people living there, especially the children.

Did you know? Reporters are very important! They help us learn about our world and can sometimes help people by telling their stories.

A Phone That Was Silent

For a very, very long time, Mohamed was in a place where it was very hard to get news out. He was stuck, and it was dangerous. His phone, which he uses to talk to people and send his stories, was quiet. It was turned off.

Can you imagine if your mom or dad’s phone was off for three whole years? No calls, no messages, no happy pictures. Nothing. That's how long Mohamed's phone was silent during this big, sad fight.

It must have been a very lonely and scary time for Mohamed and for many other people in Sudan who couldn't talk to their families or friends.

Did you know? Phones and the internet help us stay connected to our family and friends, especially when we can't see them or when they are far away.

Three Years of News, All at Once!

Then, one amazing day, something special happened. Mohamed was able to get his phone working again! Can you imagine how surprised he must have been?

Suddenly, all the messages that people had sent him, all the news stories and pictures from three whole years, came rushing into his phone! Beep! Beep! Beep! There were so many messages, like a waterfall of information!

It was like a big, big river of news flowing into his phone all at once. He saw all the sad stories, all the scary things that had happened during the big fight in Sudan while his phone was quiet.

When he saw all of it, it made him feel very, very sad. He saw how much had been lost during those three years.

Did you know? Sometimes, when news comes all at once, it can feel like a lot to understand. That's why reporters help us break it down into smaller, easier pieces.

What Does 'Lost' Mean Here?

When Mohamed saw what was lost, he wasn't talking about losing a toy or a game under the bed. He was talking about much bigger things that are very important for families and children.

People lost their safe homes. Their houses were broken or they had to leave them. They lost their schools where children used to learn, draw, and play with their friends. They lost their towns where they lived happily. Some people even lost their family members or friends, which is the saddest thing of all.

The big fight, the war, makes it very hard for children to go to school, or for families to get yummy food and clean water. It makes it hard to play outside safely without being scared.

Did you know? Every child in the world deserves a safe home, a school to learn in, and clean water to drink.

Sudan: A Place of Wonders

Sudan is actually a wonderful country with kind people and a very, very old history. Before the big fight, children there loved to play games, just like you! They went to school and learned new things every day. Families cooked delicious food and spent happy times together.

Sudan is also home to amazing pyramids, even more than in Egypt! Imagine seeing those tall, pointy buildings that are so old, they were built when your great-great-great-grandparents were little!

Did you know? Sudan has a very long river called the Nile, just like many other African countries. This big river helps plants grow and gives water to many animals and people!

Hoping for Peace and Play

Mohamed, the reporter, now has many important stories to tell the world. He wants everyone to know about the kind and strong people of Sudan and how much they want the big fight to stop.

Children in Sudan want to go back to school. They want to play with their friends without being scared. They want their families to be safe and happy again. More than anything, they want peace.

Peace means everyone can be friends and help each other. It means no more fighting, no more sadness, and everyone can live happily and safely in their homes and towns.

We can all send our kindest thoughts and wishes to the children and families in Sudan. We can hope that the big fight stops very, very soon, and that peace returns to their beautiful country quickly.

Did you know? When people work together and choose kindness, wonderful things can happen, and peace can grow even in the toughest places!

Let's wish for a bright, peaceful, and playful future for all our friends in Sudan. We are all part of the big African family!
summary: A brave reporter named Mohamed in Sudan received three years' worth of news about a big fight (war) all at once when his phone finally turned on, making him very sad about all that was lost.
whyMatters: This story helps us understand that a big fight in Sudan has made life very hard for children and families, and it reminds us to hope for peace so everyone can be safe and happy again.
